events.title: Morning Mobility
events.subtitle: Wake Up & Move
events.description: Start the week with intention. This morning flow targets the hips, thoracic spine, and shoulders — the three areas that take the most abuse from sitting and sleeping. Move slowly, breathe deeply, and set the tone for the week ahead.

exercises.name: Sun Salutation A
exercises.notes: Flow with breath. Inhale up, exhale fold.

exercises.name: Hip 90/90 Stretch
exercises.notes: Sit tall, don't round the lower back.

exercises.name: Thoracic Rotation
exercises.notes: Keep hips square to the floor.

exercises.name: Neck Circles
exercises.notes: Slow and controlled.

exercises.name: Box Breathing
exercises.notes: Inhale 4, hold 4, exhale 4, hold 4.

events.title: Upper Body Strength
events.subtitle: Push Focus — Chest & Shoulders
events.description: Heavy push day. The focus is on compound pressing movements to build raw strength in the chest, shoulders, and triceps. Keep rest periods strict — 90 seconds between working sets. The goal today is to add 5 lbs to last week's bench.

warmup.name: Band Pull-Aparts
warmup.notes: Keep arms straight, squeeze shoulder blades.

exercises.name: Barbell Bench Press
exercises.notes: Feet flat, arch controlled, bar to lower chest.

exercises.name: Overhead Press
exercises.notes: Brace core hard, press through the ceiling.

exercises.name: Cable Chest Fly
exercises.notes: Full stretch at the bottom, squeeze at the top.

exercises.name: Face Pulls
exercises.notes: Shoulder health non-negotiable — don't skip.

events.title: Nightly Recovery
events.subtitle: End-of-Day Unwind
events.description: The hardest part of training isn't the workouts — it's the recovery. This nightly sequence targets the posterior chain and chest after a heavy push day, helping flush lactic acid and improve sleep quality.

exercises.name: Supine Hamstring Stretch
exercises.notes: Use a strap or towel. Don't force the knee straight.

exercises.name: Pigeon Pose
exercises.notes: Let the hip melt into the floor.

exercises.name: Chest Opener (Floor)
exercises.notes: Arms in goalpost. Let gravity do the work.

exercises.name: Supine Spinal Twist
exercises.notes: Both shoulders stay on the ground.

events.title: Climbing Session
events.subtitle: Technique & Project Work
events.description: Tuesday evenings at the gym. Warm up on slabs and easy traversing, then move into your 5.11 project. Focus on footwork precision and reading sequences before pulling on. The goal this session is to link the crux section on the overhang problem.

warmup.name: Easy Traversing
warmup.notes: Slab and vertical only. No overhang until warm.

warmup.name: Finger Flexor Warm-Up
warmup.notes: Half-crimp position. Stop if anything pops.

exercises.name: 5.9 Routes — Volume Laps
exercises.notes: Focus on quiet feet and straight arms.

exercises.name: 5.10b/c Routes
exercises.notes: Read the route from the ground before starting.

exercises.name: 5.11a Project
exercises.notes: Rest 10+ minutes between attempts. Film one attempt for review.

exercises.name: Hangboard — Max Hangs
exercises.notes: Half-crimp, 20mm edge.

cooldown.name: Forearm Antagonist Work
cooldown.notes: Rubber band finger extensions. Injury prevention.

events.title: Nightly Recovery
events.subtitle: Post-Climb Forearm & Hip Work
events.description: After climbing, the forearms and fingers need specific attention. This version of the nightly routine adds dedicated forearm and finger flexor work alongside the standard hip and back sequence.

exercises.name: Prayer Stretch (Wrist Flexion)
exercises.notes: Palms together, press down. Feel it in the forearms.

exercises.name: Child's Pose with Lat Stretch
exercises.notes: Walk hands to each side to hit the lats.

events.title: Morning Mobility
events.subtitle: Midweek Reset
events.description: Midweek morning flow. Body is carrying some fatigue — move mindfully and don't push range of motion. The goal is circulation and mental clarity, not flexibility gains.

exercises.name: Cat-Cow
exercises.notes: Sync with breath.

exercises.name: World's Greatest Stretch
exercises.notes: Step into lunge, rotate, reach sky.

events.title: Lower Body Strength
events.subtitle: Squat & Posterior Chain
events.description: The king of leg days. Heavy back squats are the centerpiece — everything else supports and complements them. Posterior chain work (RDL, GHD) ensures balanced development and protects the knees. This is the session that builds the base for everything else.

exercises.name: Back Squat
exercises.notes: High bar, break parallel, knees track toes.

exercises.name: Romanian Deadlift
exercises.notes: Push hips back, bar stays close to legs.

exercises.name: Bulgarian Split Squat
exercises.notes: Front foot far enough forward to stay vertical shin.

exercises.name: Leg Press
exercises.notes: High foot placement for glute emphasis.

exercises.name: Standing Calf Raises
exercises.notes: Full range — all the way down, pause at top.

cooldown.name: Couch Stretch
cooldown.notes: Hip flexors are tight after heavy squats.

events.title: Nightly Recovery
events.subtitle: Post-Leg Day Deep Tissue
events.description: Legs are cooked after squats and RDLs. Extra time on the quads and hip flexors tonight. The goal is to reduce DOMS tomorrow morning.

events.title: Zone 2 Run
events.subtitle: Aerobic Base Building
events.description: Zone 2 running builds the aerobic engine that underpins all other training. Keep your heart rate between 130–145 BPM — you should be able to hold a conversation. If you can't, slow down. Consistency over weeks is what moves the needle here.

exercises.name: Zone 2 Run
exercises.notes: Target HR: 130–145 BPM. Pace is secondary — let HR dictate speed.

events.title: Nightly Recovery
events.subtitle: Post-Run Leg Reset
events.description: Standard nightly sequence with extra calf and IT band work after the run.

events.title: Morning Mobility
events.subtitle: Pre-Climb Activation
events.description: Friday morning — climbing tonight. This version of the morning routine includes shoulder and finger prep to set up a great evening session.

exercises.name: Shoulder CARs
exercises.notes: Controlled articular rotations. Full range, zero compensation.

events.title: Climbing Session
events.subtitle: Volume Day — Movement Quality
events.description: Second climb of the week. Today is less about projecting and more about volume and movement quality. Pick routes that feel moderate and focus on eliminating bad habits — don't barn-door, use your feet, and keep your hips close to the wall on overhangs.

warmup.name: Easy Traversing
warmup.notes: Footwork only. Move feet three times for every hand move.

exercises.name: 5.9 Routes — Footwork Drills
exercises.notes: Climb with one hand, other behind back on easy sections.

exercises.name: 5.10a/b Routes — Volume
exercises.notes: No falls. Downclimb when possible.

exercises.name: 5.10d Route — Onsight Attempt
exercises.notes: Read thoroughly from the ground. Commit to sequences.

exercises.name: Circuit Board — Pull Strength
exercises.notes: Bent-arm pulling. Feet on the board.

events.title: Nightly Recovery
events.subtitle: End of Week Wind-Down
events.description: End of a big week. Tonight's sequence is unhurried — hold each pose longer, breathe slower. Let the body fully absorb five days of training.

exercises.name: Legs Up the Wall
exercises.notes: Passive. Let gravity drain the legs.

exercises.name: Savasana / Body Scan
exercises.notes: Lie still. Systematically relax every muscle group.

events.title: Pull Day
events.subtitle: Back & Biceps
events.description: Weekend morning lift — the reward for a disciplined week. Pull day targets the back and biceps. After two climbing sessions, the lats are already primed. Use that mind-muscle connection and go heavier than you think you can.

exercises.name: Weighted Pull-Ups
exercises.notes: Dead hang start, chin over bar finish.

exercises.name: Barbell Row
exercises.notes: Hinge at 45°, row to lower sternum.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Curl
exercises.notes: Supinate at the top.

events.title: Nightly Recovery
events.description: Standard nightly recovery sequence.

events.title: Active Recovery
events.subtitle: Rest & Regenerate
events.description: Rest day is not an off day — it's when adaptation happens. Today: walk outside, eat well, hydrate aggressively, and get 8+ hours of sleep tonight. The only structured movement is a short walk and the nightly stretch.

exercises.name: 20-Minute Walk
exercises.notes: Outside if possible. No pace target — just move.

exercises.name: Hydration Goal
exercises.notes: 3L of water minimum.

events.title: Nightly Recovery
events.description: End of the recovery day. Full body stretch — take your time.

events.title: Morning Mobility
events.subtitle: Week 2 Kickoff
events.description: New week. Same commitment. Morning mobility resets the nervous system and gets you out of your head before the day begins.

events.title: Upper Body Strength
events.subtitle: Push Focus — Week 2
events.description: Push day week 2. Target 190 lbs on bench — 5 lb progression from last Monday. Everything else stays the same weight until the primary lift moves.

exercises.name: Barbell Bench Press
exercises.notes: +5 lbs from last week. Grind if needed.

events.title: Nightly Recovery
events.description: Standard nightly recovery sequence.

events.title: Climbing Session
events.subtitle: Project Day — Push Your Limit
events.description: Tuesday project session. This week's goal: send the 5.11a that's been a nemesis. Come in rested and mentally sharp. Visualize the crux sequence before you rope up.

exercises.name: 5.9 Routes
exercises.notes: Warm-up only. Don't fatigue.

exercises.name: 5.11a Project — Send Attempt
exercises.notes: Rest fully between attempts. This is the day.

exercises.name: Hangboard — Repeaters
exercises.notes: 20mm edge, half crimp.

events.title: Nightly Recovery
events.description: Post-climb recovery. Forearms and fingers first tonight.

events.title: Morning Mobility
events.description: Midweek mobility. Keep it gentle — heavy legs coming this afternoon.

events.title: Lower Body Strength
events.subtitle: Squat — Week 2
events.description: Back squats week 2: 230 lbs. Romanian deadlifts same weight, everything else holds. Posterior chain is the priority.

exercises.name: Back Squat
exercises.notes: +5 lbs. Stay patient between sets.

events.title: Zone 2 Run
events.subtitle: Aerobic Base — Week 2
events.description: Same run, same zone. The gains compound invisibly over weeks. Trust the process.

events.title: Climbing Session
events.subtitle: Movement Quality Day
events.description: Volume and movement quality. No projecting tonight — focus on body positioning and reading routes. Every session where you prioritize technique over sending is an investment.

exercises.name: Bouldering V1–V3
exercises.notes: Problem-solve. Explore movement.

events.title: Pull Day
events.subtitle: Back & Biceps — Week 2
events.description: Pull day week 2. Weighted pull-ups: 40 lbs. The back responds to volume — squeeze every rep.

events.title: Active Recovery
events.subtitle: Rest & Regenerate
events.description: Rest day. Walk, hydrate, eat well, sleep. The body adapts when you're not training.

events.title: Morning Mobility
events.description: Week 3 opens. Three weeks of consistency builds a habit. The morning routine should feel automatic by now.

events.title: Upper Body Strength
events.subtitle: Push Focus — Week 3
events.description: Bench target: 195 lbs. Three weeks of linear progression. If this goes up, everything is working.

exercises.name: Barbell Bench Press
exercises.notes: If set 4 is a grind, hold weight here next week.

exercises.name: Overhead Press
exercises.notes: +5 lbs on OHP this week.

events.title: Climbing Session
events.subtitle: Crack & Slab Day
events.description: Step outside your comfort zone. Spend the majority of tonight on crack and slab terrain — both require completely different technique than steep sport routes. Uncomfortable = growth.

exercises.name: Slab Routes 5.8–5.10a
exercises.notes: Trust your feet. Look down, not up. Tiny holds count.

exercises.name: Hand Crack Practice
exercises.notes: Thumb down, rotate wrist, cam the hand. Pain is temporary.

exercises.name: Campus Board — Low Rungs
exercises.notes: Explosive pull, controlled movement.

events.title: Lower Body Strength
events.subtitle: Squat — Week 3
events.description: 235 lbs on the bar. Every plate added here is real strength. The movement is the same — just heavier.

events.title: Zone 2 Run
events.subtitle: Aerobic Base — Week 3
events.description: Third consecutive week of Zone 2 work. The body is beginning to adapt — you may notice your pace at the same HR is starting to creep up. That's the aerobic engine getting built.

exercises.name: Zone 2 Run
exercises.notes: HR: 130–145 BPM. Note your pace at the 30-min mark.

events.title: Climbing Session
events.subtitle: Outdoor Prep — Movement Reading
events.description: Prep session for an upcoming outdoor day. Focus on reading routes from a distance, efficiency of movement, and building confidence on vertical terrain that will translate to rock.

exercises.name: 5.10d Onsight Practice
exercises.notes: Read from the ground. No beta from others.

exercises.name: Bouldering V2–V4
exercises.notes: Try hard on movement problems. Fall is fine.

events.title: Yoga & Mobility
events.subtitle: Full Body Flow
events.description: Replacing the Saturday lift this week with a full-length yoga and mobility session. After three heavy weeks, the body benefits from a different kind of stress — controlled movement through full range of motion, breath synchronization, and parasympathetic activation.

exercises.name: Standing Flow Sequence
exercises.notes: Warrior 1, 2, Triangle, Half Moon, Reverse Warrior

exercises.name: Hip Opening Sequence
exercises.notes: Lizard, Pigeon, Yin hold

exercises.name: Supine Cool-Down
exercises.notes: Bridge, Reclined Pigeon, Spinal Twist, Savasana

events.title: Active Recovery
events.description: End of week 3. Rest, reflect on progress, prepare for week 4.
